title: Almost all economies have increased their Internet access since 2015
subtitle: Individuals using the Internet (% of population)
source: International Telecommunication Union, ITU DataHub and World Bank staff calculations, based on Mahler et al. 2026
visdescription: This chart uses arrows to show Internet access levels by economy in 2015 and 2024. It also shows where the economies would have been, had they followed the typical pace of progress since 2015.

scenes.text: By 2024, in 54 countries, more than 90 percent of the population was using the Internet.

Countries like [emphasis: Cambodia]and the[emphasis: Kyrgyz Republic]had made much more progress by 2024 than expected based on their access levels in 2015. In [emphasis: Cambodia], Internet access levels rose from less than 7 percent to close to 70 percent of the population. The [emphasis: Kyrgyz Republic]also made remarkable progress, from 30 percent of the population in 2015 to over 90 percent by 2024.

_meta.approvalNote: hand layouts; coverage_map is ranked-bars proxy not Mapbox
